releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Initial release

Interstitial Journal provides quick commands for [interstitial journalling](https://nesslabs.com/interstitial-journaling) in your daily notes. Insert timestamped entries with a keyboard shortcut instead of typing them out by hand.

### Commands

- Add interstitial journal entry -- inserts a timestamped entry (e.g. `- 14:30 -`) and leaves the cursor ready to type.
- Add interstitial journal entry with new page -- inserts a timestamped entry with a wiki link (e.g. `- 14:30 - [[2026-02-26 ]]`), with the cursor placed inside the brackets.

### Smart line detection

Both commands adapt to the current line context:

- Empty lines get the entry inserted in place.
- Bare list markers (auto-continued bullets) are reused rather than doubled up.
- Lines with existing content get the entry on a new line below, preserving indentation.

### Configurable settings

- Time format -- any Moment.js format string (default `HH:mm`).
- Date format -- format for the date in new-page wiki links (default `YYYY-MM-DD`).
- Time style -- normal or bold.
- Divider -- hyphen, colon, arrow, slash, pipe, or none.
- Entry format -- bullet list or plain text.
